channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The second shoe is about to drop on a big DoD cybersecurity program
item.description: The DoD’s big cybersecurity program advanced earlier this month. A big rule to carry if out became effective.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: An online debate over foreign workers in tech shows tensions in Trump's political coalition
item.description: An online spat between factions of Donald Trump’s supporters over immigration and the tech industry has thrown internal divisions in the president-elect’s political movement into public display.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity
item.description: On its latest deployment, the Nimitz-class carrier went from slow military satellite uplinks to modern capabilities that move terabytes of data each day. The post Lincoln returns to port after proving out ‘game changing’ connectivity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Mexico tests cellphone app allowing migrants to send alert if they are about to be detained in US
item.description: Mexico is developing a cellphone app that will allow migrants to warn relatives and consulates if they think they are about to be detained by U.S. immigration officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 14 new California laws to watch for in 2025
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom and lawmakers passed big changes to laws this year, and many go into effect in 2025. Here's a list of some of the most impactful.
item.dc:creator: Grant Stringer, The Mercury News

item.title: Sign PDFs easily in Preview on Apple devices
item.description: With electronic signatures becoming more common, Kurt the CyberGuy explains how to produce your digital John Hancock for online documents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: I tried using Alexa and smart home devices to help fix my irregular routine – and it changed my life
item.description: Smart home devices are useful for many reasons, but for me, it’s all about getting my life in order. Here’s how automations and clever smart assistant features helped me fix my routine.
item.dc:creator: josephine.watson@futurenet.com (Josephine Watson), TechRadar

item.title: Security leaders don't want to be held personally liable for attacks
item.description: Some security workers don’t want the personal liability consequences that come with CISO and other leadership roles.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The Special Olympics embarks on digital journey to empower its athletes
item.description: The Special Olympics is overhauling its technology stack as part of a digital transformation designed to bolster the training of its athletes across the globe.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cómo trabaja Ikea con la IA: 30.000 empleados formados
item.description: Como la mayoría de las empresas, Ikea se centra en los datos y la inteligencia artificial (IA) para cumplir sus ambiciones de futuro. Por ello, hace cuatro años, la empresa desarrolló una estrategia de datos para todo el grupo, que se ha seguido desde entonces.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Roy, Cruz named most active X posters in Congress: Report
item.description: Rep. Chip Roy (R-Texas) and Sen. Ted Cruz (R-Texas) were named the most active posters on the social platform X this year among all lawmakers in Congress, according to a recent report. The Dec.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: 2024 review: ‘Typhoons’ bookend busy year in cyber
item.description: From Volt Typhoon to Salt Typhoon, major cyber incidents in 2024 shined a spotlight on how agencies are managing cyber threats to critical infrastructure. The post 2024 review: ‘Typhoons’ bookend busy year in cyber first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Samsung tipped to unveil the Galaxy Ring 2 at its January Unpacked event
item.description: It looks as though there are going to be plenty of new gadgets to talk about at the January 22 showcase.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Nvidia in 2024: year in review
item.description: This year, Nvidia’s value went through the roof, and new RTX Super GPUs were definite crowd-pleasers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Japan Airlines was hit by a cyberattack, delaying flights during the year-end holiday season
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan Airlines said it was hit by a cyberattack Thursday, causing delays to more than 20 domestic flights but the carrier said it was able to stop the onslaught and restore its systems hours later. There was no impact on flight safety, it said.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Key Tech and Workforce Provisions in the 2025 Intel Authorization Bill
item.description: The fiscal 2025 intelligence authorization act includes a range of provisions, from workforce development to assessments of China's biotechnology efforts.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: What is serverless? Serverless computing explained
item.description: Over the past two decades, considerable innovation has gone into making sure developers don’t have to think much about the underlying physical machine their code runs on. Serverless computing is a paradigm that takes this objective to its logical conclusion.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Baby mammoth preserved for 50,000 years is unveiled in Russia's Siberia
item.description: The 50,000-year-old remains of a baby mammoth uncovered by melting permafrost have been unveiled to the public by researchers in Russia’s Siberia region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Los clientes de SAP están tardando en implantar la IA de forma generalizada
item.description: Una nueva encuesta realizada por SAP a organizaciones clientes de las soluciones de la compañía muestra que, a pesar de experimentar con la IA, pocas han implementado esta tecnología (incuyendo la modalidad generativa) en sus empresas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: We used Google’s AI to analyze 188 predictions of what’s in store for tech in 2025
item.description: At this time of year investment banks, advertising agencies, and seemingly every other business on the planet share their predictions on what is likely to unfold in the next 12 months. Journalists’ inboxes sag under the weight of unsolicited predictions for the year ahead.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Top 5 mistakes that could expose your financial data to cybercriminals
item.description: There are common habits and oversights that could leave your financial information vulnerable to cybercriminals, according to Kurt the CyberGuy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union continued a strike Friday at seven of the company's delivery hubs just days before Christmas. At midnight on Saturday, workers at a prominent unionized warehouse in New York will also join, the Teamsters said.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More CISA staff would keep working under DHS’ revised shutdown plan
item.description: Under a new plan, about one-third of CISA's staff will keep working through a shutdown. But experts are still concerned about the impact on U.S. cyber defenses. The post More CISA staff would keep working under DHS’ revised shutdown plan first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network
